title: Europe Today: EU fails to agree on trade deal with US, risking Trump backlash
content: Tune in to Europe Today, Euronews' flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.
snippet: Tune in to Europe Today, Euronews' flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: DMA: How the EU’s big tech law changed citizens’ lives
content: Under the DMA, Europeans can choose their default browser, pay with rival apps, and message across platforms. Enforcement remains a work in progress, and many citizens do not know the law exists.
snippet: Under the DMA, Europeans can choose their default browser, pay with rival apps, and message across platforms. Enforcement remains a work in progress, and many citizens do not know the law exists.

title: How to avoid a trade war with China? MEPs debate on The Ring
content: Is a trade war with China looming? In this edition of our weekly debate show The Ring, Sakis Arnaoutoglou (S&D) and Nicolás de la Parte (EPP) discuss what could be one of the most consequential topics on the EU's political agenda.

title: Commissioner vows to end poverty in 25 years as EU unveils first major strategy
content: “We want to eradicate poverty in the EU in 25 years,” European Commission Vice-President Roxana Mînzatu told Euronews in an interview. However, the anti-poverty strategy lacks a dedicated budget and relies mainly on recommendations rather than binding legislation.

title: Spain ‘more convinced of its own position’ on the US than the EU is, Jaume Duch tells Euronews
content: The Catalan regional minister told Euronews that, while Spain has been leading when it comes to standing up against Washington, the EU has taken a firmer stance in recent months.
snippet: The Catalan regional minister told Euronews that, while Spain has been leading when it comes to standing up against Washington, the EU has taken a firmer stance in recent months.

title: Divisions in Brussels as EU negotiators face crunch talks on US trade deal
content: MEPs and EU countries are facing growing pressure just hours before key talks on implementing the EU–US trade deal. However, officials warn that the Parliament’s demands may be too ambitious to secure an agreement this evening.
snippet: MEPs and EU countries are facing growing pressure just hours before key talks on implementing the EU–US trade deal. However, officials warn that the Parliament’s demands may be too ambitious to secure

title: French carrier group moves south of Suez ahead of conditional UK-French Hormuz mission
content: The carrier’s southward move puts French air assets within range of the strait without entering the Gulf, where the US Navy has been blockading Iranian ports since 13 April.
snippet: The carrier’s southward move puts French air assets within range of the strait without entering the Gulf, where the US Navy has been blockading Iranian ports since 13 April.

title: Portugal arrests further 15 police officers in ongoing Lisbon rape and torture probe
content: According to the Portuguese press, the victims were mainly undocumented foreigners, homeless people or drug users.
snippet: According to the Portuguese press, the victims were mainly undocumented foreigners, homeless people or drug users.

title: Son of Myanmar's ousted leader appeals to Macron for help in securing proof of life
content: The military's 2021 takeover prompted widespread public protests, whose violent suppression by security forces triggered an armed resistance that has now led to a state of civil war.
snippet: The military's 2021 takeover prompted widespread public protests, whose violent suppression by security forces triggered an armed resistance that has now led to a state of civil war.

title: EU trade chief urges US to ‘swiftly’ restore 15% tariff arrangement
content: EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič met US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer ahead of what is expected to be a tense round of negotiations between EU countries and MEPs over the implementation of the EU-US trade deal, as Washington pushes the EU to rapidly eliminate tariffs on US goods.

title: Kyiv slams Egypt for accepting Russian‑shipped grain stolen from Ukraine
content: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said on Tuesday that Egypt has received a fourth shipment of stolen Ukrainian grain via Russia, accusing Cairo of violating its promises.
snippet: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said on Tuesday that Egypt has received a fourth shipment of stolen Ukrainian grain via Russia, accusing Cairo of violating its promises.

title: End of conflict in the Middle East or recession in Europe, IMF warns
content: The IMF’s outlook for Europe remains closely tied to geopolitical developments. A prolonged crisis risks tipping Europe into recession.
snippet: The IMF’s outlook for Europe remains closely tied to geopolitical developments. A prolonged crisis risks tipping Europe into recession.

title: Russia unilaterally declares Victory Day ceasefire while Zelenskyy tables own truce
content: Russia's defence ministry said last week that there would be no military hardware at the annual parade this year because of fears of long-range attacks by Ukrainian drones.
snippet: Russia's defence ministry said last week that there would be no military hardware at the annual parade this year because of fears of long-range attacks by Ukrainian drones.

title: More than 20 bystanders killed in Sweden gangland shootings in three years, police say
content: The innocent victims include people hit by stray bullets, cases of a mistaken identity by the shooter and relatives of targeted gang members, according to police statistics.
snippet: The innocent victims include people hit by stray bullets, cases of a mistaken identity by the shooter and relatives of targeted gang members, according to police statistics.

title: Austria expels three Russian Embassy staff over suspected antenna spying in Vienna
content: The Russian Embassy said in a statement on Monday that it had taken note of Austria's "outrageous" decision concerning its employees.
snippet: The Russian Embassy said in a statement on Monday that it had taken note of Austria's "outrageous" decision concerning its employees.

title: At least one killed after car drives into people on street in German city of Leipzig
content: Leipzig is located southwest of Berlin and has more than 630,000 inhabitants, making it one of the biggest cities in eastern Germany.
snippet: Leipzig is located southwest of Berlin and has more than 630,000 inhabitants, making it one of the biggest cities in eastern Germany.

title: Four migrants found dead near Croatia-Slovenia border, police say
content: More than 12,500 people used the so-called Balkan route in 2025, according to data from the European border agency, Frontex.
snippet: More than 12,500 people used the so-called Balkan route in 2025, according to data from the European border agency, Frontex.

title: Professor expelled from university for inventing 'fake Nobel prize' and awarding it to himself
content: A lecturer-researcher from Besançon is at the centre of a scandal rocking academia: he is suspected of having fabricated an international philology award in order to give it to himself. The ruse has seen him expelled from his university.

title: Two drones from Russia crash in Latvia with one causing oil depot fire, army says
content: Europe is on high alert after drone flyovers into NATO airspace reached an unprecedented scale last September, prompting leaders to agree to develop a "drone wall."
snippet: Europe is on high alert after drone flyovers into NATO airspace reached an unprecedented scale last September, prompting leaders to agree to develop a "drone wall."

title: Iran could strangle Strait in future crisis, ex-Trump envoy warns
content: After more than two months of war between the US and Iran, a deal appears in sight to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's vital shipping routes. However, former Trump official Kurt Volker says the conflict has effectively handed control of the Strait to Tehran.

title: The Italian maxi-plant changing the energy balance in Libya
content: Eni's maxi module starts from Ravenna to Libya: less waste, more gas and an increasingly strategic Italy-Libya axis in the Mediterranean
snippet: Eni's maxi module starts from Ravenna to Libya: less waste, more gas and an increasingly strategic Italy-Libya axis in the Mediterranean

title: White House calls Europe 'incubator' for terrorism in new counter-terrorism strategy
content: The fresh criticism of Europe comes just months after Trump's new national security strategy said the continent faced "civilisational erasure" due to immigration.
snippet: The fresh criticism of Europe comes just months after Trump's new national security strategy said the continent faced "civilisational erasure" due to immigration.

title: French right-wing broadcaster shares fake magazine cover of French National Assembly president
content: Political commentators mocked Yaël Braun-Pivet, president of France’s National Assembly, for featuring on the cover of tabloid magazine "Closer" on 4 May, except that the image was made by AI.
snippet: Political commentators mocked Yaël Braun-Pivet, president of France’s National Assembly, for featuring on the cover of tabloid magazine "Closer" on 4 May, except that the image was made by AI.

title: Voting under way in UK local elections seen as a verdict on Keir Starmer's leadership
content: Local elections usually focus on issues like rubbish collection, graffiti and potholes but Starmer’s opponents have painted Thursday's vote as a referendum on the prime minister.
snippet: Local elections usually focus on issues like rubbish collection, graffiti and potholes but Starmer’s opponents have painted Thursday's vote as a referendum on the prime minister.

title: MEPs and EU countries fail to reach an agreement on EU-US deal
content: Despite intense pressure from the US, negotiators agreed to meet again in 10 days after more than 6 hours of talks last night. Both sides, seeking a compromise, said however that progress had been made during the negotiations.

title: From the economy to culture: What makes Europeans proud?
content: Renaissance art in Italy, democracy in Sweden and people in Spain... Europeans define national pride in unique ways.
snippet: Renaissance art in Italy, democracy in Sweden and people in Spain... Europeans define national pride in unique ways.

title: Brussels makes progress but no breakthrough on EU-US trade deal
content: Negotiators from the European Parliament and European Council faced-off Wednesday night to aiming to conclude the EU-US trade deal. Despite the lengthy discussion and fears of a potential US retaliation, no end was brokered, with talks continuing on 19 May.
snippet: Negotiators from the European Parliament and European Council faced-off Wednesday night to aiming to conclude the EU-US trade deal. Despite the lengthy discussion and fears of a potential US retaliati

title: Zelenskyy hails 'important step' as Hungary returns assets seized from Ukraine bank workers
content: Ukraine's Oschadbank demanded the immediate return of cash and gold confiscated by Hungarian authorities in March, lawyers representing the bank in Hungary told Euronews.
snippet: Ukraine's Oschadbank demanded the immediate return of cash and gold confiscated by Hungarian authorities in March, lawyers representing the bank in Hungary told Euronews.

title: 'Far from united': Mélenchon's 2027 candidacy does not appeal to French left outside his party
content: The alliances forged with Mélenchon's LFI in the run-up to the municipal elections have already been criticised by some centre-left voices. Now, the idea of a left-wing primary is gaining traction.
snippet: The alliances forged with Mélenchon's LFI in the run-up to the municipal elections have already been criticised by some centre-left voices. Now, the idea of a left-wing primary is gaining traction.

title: 'We had to fight a different kind of mafia,' Magyar says ahead of talks with Meloni
content: The lack of information about what the leaders will discuss has led to speculation about how relations between Budapest and Rome might look after 16 years of Viktor Orbán's government.
snippet: The lack of information about what the leaders will discuss has led to speculation about how relations between Budapest and Rome might look after 16 years of Viktor Orbán's government.

title: Hard-right Reform UK party eyes huge new gains in local elections on Thursday
content: Reform UK has surged in polls largely due to its campaign against immigration and has vowed a wave of mass deportations if it wins the next general election in 2029.
snippet: Reform UK has surged in polls largely due to its campaign against immigration and has vowed a wave of mass deportations if it wins the next general election in 2029.

title: EU countries need more 'targeted measures' to tackle soaring energy prices, says IMF's Helge Berger
content: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) said vulnerable households should be the main target of energy price relief measures and urged EU governments to guarantee that these won't do "more damage than good".

title: Deadly air: Which European countries have the worst PM 2.5 levels?
content: Up to 20% of monitoring stations in Europe recorded air pollution levels above the current EU air quality standards.
snippet: Up to 20% of monitoring stations in Europe recorded air pollution levels above the current EU air quality standards.

title: Meloni slams AI-generated images of herself, calling deepfakes a 'dangerous tool'
content: Doctored sexualised images of the prime minister have surfaced before, particularly last year on a pornographic website that included altered images of high-profile women.
snippet: Doctored sexualised images of the prime minister have surfaced before, particularly last year on a pornographic website that included altered images of high-profile women.

title: Russia broke unilateral ceasefire with drone and missile attacks, Ukraine's FM says

title: EU squares up Trump’s tariff threat
content: As tensions flare between Russia and Ukraine over competing ceasefires, and the Iran war shows no signs of ending, politicians in Brussels will spend Wednesday searching for a way to thrash out the final details of the EU-US trade agreement, lest they suffer more import levy threats from Trump.

title: How is the DMA reshaping Big Tech's grip on the internet?
content: Two years in, Europe's landmark law has changed how Big Tech operates and given users more freedom and choice, but the battle is far from over. Watch the video!

title: Meloni -Trump tensions tested, can the US - Italy alliance hold?
content: Tensions between Meloni and Trump do not seem to threaten the privileged axis between Rome and Washington, shored up by US bases in Italy and Italian contracts.
snippet: Tensions between Meloni and Trump do not seem to threaten the privileged axis between Rome and Washington, shored up by US bases in Italy and Italian contracts.

title: Ukraine strikes Russian military facility more than 1,000 kilometres into Moscow's territory
content: Ukraine launched a long-range strike against Russia’s military site over 1,000 km from its border, just days before Moscow’s Victory Day parade.
snippet: Ukraine launched a long-range strike against Russia’s military site over 1,000 km from its border, just days before Moscow’s Victory Day parade.

title: Romania's pro-EU coalition collapses after prime minister fails no-confidence vote
content: Romania has faced a long period of instability and the country is grappling with one of the highest budget deficits in the EU, rampant inflation and a technical recession.
snippet: Romania has faced a long period of instability and the country is grappling with one of the highest budget deficits in the EU, rampant inflation and a technical recession.

title: Can US law stop Trump from withdrawing troops from Europe?
content: A 2026 US defence law does not prevent troop withdrawals from Europe, but imposes consultations and justifications for major cuts that make such a move more difficult.
snippet: A 2026 US defence law does not prevent troop withdrawals from Europe, but imposes consultations and justifications for major cuts that make such a move more difficult.

title: Sweden announces new spy agency in rethink prompted by war in Ukraine
content: Stockholm dropped two centuries of military non-alignment and joined NATO in the wake of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
snippet: Stockholm dropped two centuries of military non-alignment and joined NATO in the wake of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

title: NATO still in the dark over US plans to withdraw 5,000 troops
content: The US has not specified to NATO which operations will be impacted as part of the Pentagon's decision to remove 5,000 soldiers from German territory. European nations have been preparing for an eventual change in US force posture, but were not consulted before this announcement.

title: No, the Netherlands didn't 'legalise illegal migration' — what the Senate really decided

title: 'A deal is a deal': Von der Leyen hits back at Trump's latest tariff threat
content: The European Union is "prepared for every scenario" if Donald Trump unilaterally hikes tariffs on EU-made cars, says Ursula von der Leyen.
